question:
Will Lance Stroll be the 2025 Drivers Champion?

Description: This market will resolve according to the driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2025 F1 season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

As soon as the results for the final scheduled race of the 2025 F1 season are known this market will resolve.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2025 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), this market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.
event_title:
Will Lance Stroll be the 2025 Drivers Champion?

Description: This market will resolve according to the driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2025 F1 season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

As soon as the results for the final scheduled race of the 2025 F1 season are known this market will resolve.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2025 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), this market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

evidences.title: The first test of whether Aston Martin's escaped upgrade 'chaos'
evidences.content: "Time will tell whether it's good enough. But nothing's ever perfect, so I'm sure it will improve. "We have a good set of data from the windtunnel with the characteristics of this aero package, and then by the end of Friday, we'll have a track-derived set of data. "Will it perfectly align? I doubt any team ever gets it to be perfectly aligned, but you end up with offsets that you're comfortable with." If the upgrade is effective, in terms of performance gain but also accuracy in correlation from tunnel to track, then it will indicate Aston Martin has finally made progress in an area it has lagged behind rivals in for far too long - something ruthlessly exposed by how much it has slumped since its podium-scoring run at the start of 2023, when others underperformed. [...] "And the way we test here at the circuit - that third world of aerodynamics, the full size car with its different stiffness characteristics compared with a windtunnel model and running on a track with kerbs and bumps and undulations and movement of the car - that's what we will learn tomorrow. "It's trying to be more thorough. Will it all stitch together? I doubt any team has 100% correlation." He added: "It all...

evidences.title: Aston Martin discusses investment and 2026 ambitions
evidences.content: Aston Martin said it has invested heavily in a new factory and wind tunnel in recent years with the aim of fighting for wins and championships under the 2026 technical regulations. The team also described structural changes intended to support its transition to a full works team and to optimize its technical partnership with Honda, Aramco and Valvoline.

evidences.title: TECH ANALYSIS on 2025 Aston Martin AMR25: Can Aston Martin threaten the podium again with more 'driveable' AMR25? | Formula 1®
evidences.content: Skip to content (opens in a new tab)(opens in a new tab)(opens in a new tab)Hospitality (opens in a new tab)Experiences (opens in a new tab)Arcade (opens in a new tab) (opens in a new tab)Sign InSubscribe 2025Aston MartinTechnical # TECH ANALYSIS: Can Aston Martin threaten the podium again with more 'driveable' AMR25? Special ContributorMark Hughes Aston Martin tech analysis header.png Aston Martin’s new AMR25 appears to be a further smoothed-out interpretation of last year’s car, with essentially the same chassis dimensions and similar running gear (albeit with tweaked suspension geometry). Created under the supervision of since-departed Technical Director Dan Fallows, the car retains its carry-over Mercedes rear gearbox, wheelbase dimensions and cockpit positioning. [...] There is an all-new floor which, say Aston, “has been revised to improve airflow underneath the car. The new sidepod and bodywork design complement this, allowing better airflow management under the car and over the rear wing.” A lot of attention has been focused upon the rear wing’s behaviour in the transient stages of a corner, as the car is turned in. Excessive yaw in this early phase of the corner was bel...

evidences.title: Future regulations across multiple categories confirmed during ... - FIA
evidences.content: Welfare of the individual Equality and inclusion Accountability Confidentiality Regulatory updates FIA Formula One World Championship Minor changes for 2024 and 2025 Sporting and Technical regulations were approved by the World Council, including the removal of the point awarded for setting the fastest lap and the increase in requirement for fielding a young driver during free practice from once per season to two times per season. FIA World Rally Championship [...] With regard to championship points, the World Council approved an update to allocate two points to all registered competitors classified beyond 15th position, and one point to all registered competitors who are not classified in a Rally-Raid event (four points and two points respectively for Marathon Rally-Raids). This aims to provide a small incentive to registered competitors, ease tie-breaks, and facilitate the monitoring of participation. This proposal has also been applied to the World and Baja Cups. FIA Karting The World Council approved the 2025 karting calendar, which includes the addition of two new FIA competitions: an FIA Karting Academy Trophy for Senior drivers at three rounds and an FIA World Cup for the...

evidences.title: Aston Martin announces new Stroll F1 deal "for 2025 and beyond"
evidences.content: F1 British GP safety car finish defended amid fan backlash # Aston Martin announces new Stroll F1 deal "for 2025 and beyond" ## The Canadian stays on at the Silverstone-based squad for a sixth F1 season Alex Kalinauckas Lance Stroll, Aston Martin F1 Team, puts his helmet on Lance Stroll, Aston Martin F1 Team, puts his helmet on Photo by: Zak Mauger / Motorsport Images Aston Martin has announced that Lance Stroll will race on with the team "for 2025 and beyond" in what is being billed as a contract extension for the Canadian driver whose father owns the team. Stroll has raced at Aston since 2019 his father Lawrence's consortium purchased the assets of what was the then the Force India squad the following year. [...] Stroll and Alonso will now head into their third season as Aston team-mates in 2025, with their current points totals in the 2024 campaign sitting at 17 and 41 respectively. ## We want your opinion! What would you like to see on Motorsport.com? - The Motorsport.com Team #### Share Or Save This Story ### Top Comments Who is Kimi Antonelli, F1 sprint polesitter at just 18? Why Bottas has the edge over Perez in 2026 F1 return race What if Red Bull solved its second-car p...

evidences.title: Aston Martin reveals launch plan for 2025 F1 challenger
evidences.content: Both Fernando Alonso and Lance Stroll will then drive the 2025 car in a filming day at the Bahrain International Circuit, two days before pre-season testing begins in earnest at the venue. The AMR25 will be the fifth car launched by the team since it took the Aston Martin branding, following its short stint as Racing Point when Lawrence Stroll took over the team from Vijay Mallya in 2018. Following the team's heady ascent up the pecking order in 2023, when it managed to score podiums with some regularity across the year in a car that showed great race pace, 2024 brought the team back to earth. Lance Stroll, Aston Martin AMR24 Lance Stroll, Aston Martin AMR24 Photo by: Sam Bagnall / Motorsport Images [...] What is Racing Bulls doing that Red Bull isn't? evidences.title: Racing | Aston Martin USA
evidences.content: For over a century, Aston Martin has dominated the racing world, cementing its legacy in motorsport history. Since 1913, the iconic wings have graced cars that redefine performance and innovation. Decades of relentless dedication have propelled Aston Martin to victories at Le Mans and Brooklands, leading to continued success and groundbreaking partnerships in modern racing. Today, that competitive spirit drives Aston Martin forward. With a fleet of state-of-the-art racing machines, Aston Martin is primed to conquer circuits worldwide, standing as the only manufacturer competing across all levels of endurance racing and Formula 1®. ##### Explore ### Aston Martin Racing ##### Explore AMR25 ### AMF1® Scrollable block background ### Racing Cars [...] ### AMF1® Scrollable block background ### Racing Cars Our current lineup of cars competes across a wide range categories to deliver success, from Formula One™ to endurance racing. The legacy of Aston Martin continues to grow on-track. Promo ### AMR25 More drivable. More predictable. More stable. Evolution from nose to tail. The AMR25 is designed to allow Aston Martin Aramco drivers Lance Stroll and Fernando Alonso to push the limits on...

evidences.title: ‘We have a lot of work to do’ – Lance Stroll claims Aston Martin are ‘four-and-a-half seconds’ off top teams with 2026 car
evidences.content: Pushed on where that kind of lap time might come from, the Canadian added: “I don’t think it falls from the sky. You have to improve and find performance in the car and the engine. These are just usual things in F1. “When you’re behind the competition, you have to think about ways to extract more from the package you have, and at the same time also improve. No one stands still in this business. Everyone’s trying to find performance in every way, every weekend, all the time. We’re doing that. “We’re trying to extract more performance every day from the car. I think also longer term, bringing upgrades on the PU [power unit] side, on the chassis side. We will see in Australia where we line up, and then we will see throughout the season how we progress.” [...] Skip to content (opens in a new tab)(opens in a new tab)(opens in a new tab)(opens in a new tab)(opens in a new tab)(opens in a new tab) (opens in a new tab)Sign InSubscribe Lance StrollAston MartinFernando Alonso # ‘We have a lot of work to do’ – Stroll claims Aston Martin are ‘four-and-a-half seconds’ off top teams with 2026 car Aston Martin appear to be on the back foot after the first few days of pre-season action in Bahra...

evidences.title: Racing - Aston Martin - Media
evidences.content: In 2025, Aston Martin also returns to the pinnacle of endurance motorsport with Valkyrie, as it seeks to record its first overall victory in the 24 Hours of Le Mans since 1959. The Valkyrie hypercar is the only purebred competitor to contest both the FIA World Endurance Championship and the US-based IMSA WeatherTech SportsCar Championship. Moreover, as multiple GT FIA World Endurance [WEC] manufacturers’ champions and 24 Hours of Le Mans class winners, Aston Martin Racing continues to build a legacy of success with its key partners. Aston Martin builds Vantage GT3 and GT4 race cars for partners competing globally at all levels of endurance competition. Through these teams, Aston Martin Racing supports endurance campaigns at the highest level right down to domestic club competition.
